snippet.title: Lessons for Your Business Toolbox: Stanley Black & Decker President & CEO Donald Allan Jr.
snippet.description:
The Travelers Institute, the public policy division of Travelers, hosted a webinar with Stanley Black & Decker President and CEO Donald Allan Jr. The session explored what it’s like to lead a major manufacturing company in 2023. The discussion touched on supply chain, inflation, innovation, social responsibility and how today’s corporate leaders can succeed while providing value to all stakeholders.

The Travelers Institute’s webinar series explores pressing topics at the intersection of insurance, business and public policy. Learn more: https://travl.rs/3mQ3wRR.

Presented by the Travelers Institute, the MetroHartford Alliance, the Master's in Financial Technology (FinTech) Program at the University of Connecticut School of Business, the Connecticut Business & Industry Association and the Insurance Association of Connecticut.

View descriptive transcript: https://travl.rs/3nWnqLp

0:00 – Welcome.
8:24 – Create organizational change by valuing quality over quantity. 
15:33 – Allan on what makes a good leader:
20:27 – Stretch by putting yourself in uncomfortable situations. 
23:20 – The challenge of running a global manufacturing company. 
53:51 – What’s the CEO’s favorite Stanley Black & Decker product?

snippet.title: Investing in Employees: Donald Allan, Jr., President & CEO of Stanley Black & Decker
snippet.description: Business Roundtable member Donald Allan, Jr., President & Chief Executive Officer of Stanley Black & Decker, on building sustainable workforce programs: “The Workplace Partnership Initiative reflects the importance of public-private partnerships in establishing sustainable workforce programs. Business-led collaborative programs have the capacity to be agile and create scale. A more effective approach is to get everyone engaged—businesses, government, technical schools, vocational schools, community colleges, and labor—to work together to build out programs that are more responsive to the current needs.”

A more effective approach is to get everyone engaged—businesses, government, technical schools, vocational schools, community colleges, and labor—to work together to build out programs that are more responsive to the current needs.”

snippet.title: Press conference (Close of Play | Only Test) | Allan Donald, Bangladesh Fast Bowling Coach | Day 03
snippet.description:
Modhumoti Bank Limited Test Match: Bangladesh vs Ireland: Day 03

Press conference (Close of Play | Only Test) | Allan Donald, Bangladesh Fast Bowling Coach



WARNING: Unauthorized reproduction of this video is strictly prohibited. All rights reserved. Piracy is illegal and will result in criminal prosecution. Please support the creators by only sharing and viewing original content from authorized sources.

Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB) is the governing body of cricket in Bangladesh and is recognized under the National Sports Council (NSC) Act 1974, having its Head Office in Sher-e- Bangla National Cricket Stadium, Dhaka 1216, Bangladesh.

The Bangladesh national cricket team, known as The Tigers, is the national cricket team representing Bangladesh. The team is administered by the Bangladesh Cricket Board (BCB).

snippet.title: The Only Co-Star Who Truly Hated Alan Alda on M*A*S*H
snippet.description:
One of the most popular and successful American TV shows of all time is M*A*S*H. Among the cast members who became a star thanks to the show was Alan Alda, who played what became the lead role of “Hawkeye” Pierce. Alan Alda is also, without a doubt, one of the most respected actors in show business as well as one of the best-liked personalities. But not everyone liked working with Alan Alda on the set of M*A*S*H.

▬Contents of this video▬
00:00 - Intro
00:29 - Gary Burghoff 
02:25 - Mike Farrell
04:00 - Wayne Rogers
05:51 - Larry Linville
07:22 - Outro

Like this content? Subscribe here: https://www.youtube.com/factsverse?sub_confirmation=1 

Or, watch more videos here: https://www.youtube.com/playlist?list=PLkXAntdjbcSJlJnpP4FgdU0swKbnkNgJj

Actor Gary Burghoff left the show after 7 seasons. While this wasn’t said out loud, it was later revealed that many of the cast members had difficulty working with him. He particularly had a difficult relationship with Alan Alda. But he also grew tired of the show and wanted to move on to other things and wanted to focus more on his personal life.

Mike Farrell and Alan Alda were great friends but also had a friendly rivalry. At times, their friendly rivalry was able to translate on-screen and this made things interesting for their characters’ dynamic relationship. But there were times when they also had creative differences as Alan Alda was later heavily involved behind the scenes for the show.

Wayne Rogers was given a heartfelt sendoff when he passed away from Alan Alda. But the two of them had their issues as Wayne Rogers felt that his co-star got more attention at the expense of the other cast members.

As one can see, there were many behind-the-scenes issues that one wonders how the show managed to carry on for so long. Larry Linville, for example, had a reputation for being difficult to work with and this caused many issues for his co-stars and even won him the ire of fans. 

Join FactsVerse to learn about the only Co-Star Who Truly Hated Alan Alda on M*A*S*H …

The Only Co-Star Who Truly Hated Alan Alda on M*A*S*H

snippet.title: Allan Donald's contract is extended till 2023 World Cup #cricket #shorts #cricketnews
snippet.description:
#jamillatif
Allan Donald's contract is extended till 2023 World Cup #cricket #shorts #cricketnews 

#cricket 
#coach 
#coaching  
#news 
#ytshorts 
#video 
#cricketvideos 

Bangladesh pace bowling coach Allan Donald will now be available until the upcoming 2023 ODI World Cup in India in October November, Bangladesh Cricket Board told Cricbuzz on Friday.

Donald was earlier appointed as pace bowling coach of the Bangladesh in March last year and the contract was until the T20 World Cup 2022 in Australia. Later, BCB extended his contract till the home series against India.

snippet.title: Kohli 500 Not Out... | The 360 Show S02E04 (ft. Allan Donald)
snippet.description:
My friend Virat Kohli recently celebrated his 500th international game with his 76th international century. What a player and what a man he is. On the latest episode of The 360 Show presented by Fairplay, I talk about him, Australia's retention of the Ashes, and the incredible longevity of Broad and Anderson, with a special guest appearance from White Lightning himself – Allan Donald.

You can watch my full interview with Virat Kohli right here:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ONZgfsfZLmc 

The full video of Virat Kohli with Joshua Da Silva's mother, and her interview, can be found on @Vimalwa's channel. You can check it out right here: 
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=BBKsPO-7VS0

snippet.title: A Road Rage That Led to Death Row
snippet.description:
In 1998, Douglas Feldman was on a nighttime motorcycle ride in Plano, Texas, when a large truck driven by Robert Everett sped past him and abruptly entered his lane, narrowly avoiding a collision. In response, Feldman retrieved his 9mm firearm and fired several shots into the back of the truck's trailer. Subsequently, he rode his motorcycle alongside the truck's cab and opened fire, resulting in Everett's death.



Court records indicate that Feldman then circled back to ensure that Everett was deceased. On his way home, he passed an Exxon gas station and fired two rounds into the rear of a tanker truck, which was driven by Nicholas Velasquez . A week later, Feldman fatally shot Antonio Vega outside a Jack-in-the-Box, simply because Vega was in close proximity to an 18-wheeler truck.


Feldman was executed by lethal injection in Huntsville, TX on July 2013. His final words were, "I hereby declare, Robert Steven Everett and Nicholas Velasquez, guilty of crimes against me, Douglas Alan Feldman. Either by fact or by proxy, I find them both guilty. I hereby sentence both of them to death, which I carried out in August 1998. As of that time, the State of Texas has been holding me illegally in confinement and by force for 15 years. I hereby protest my pending execution and demand immediate relief."

On his way home, he passed an Exxon gas station and fired two rounds into the rear of a tanker truck, which was driven by Nicholas Velasquez . A week later, Feldman fatally shot Antonio Vega outside a Jack-in-the-Box, simply because Vega was in close proximity to an 18-wheeler truck.
